title: Annotation
$comment:
This is a type used in Stencila Schema for annotated inline content.

It extends [`Mark`](./mark.md) to attach structured authorship and
provenance-aware commentary to a span of content, rather than relying only on
presentational markup. This makes annotations useful in collaborative and
review-oriented document workflows.

Key properties include `annotation`, together with inherited metadata and
provenance features from [`Mark`](./mark.md).

analogues.notes: Approximate HTML analogue for highlighted or annotated spans, though Stencila `Annotation` additionally stores attached block content as the annotation itself.
Close JATS analogue for attached annotation content.
Closest Pandoc inline container analogue, but annotations there are usually conveyed via attributes rather than a dedicated node with block annotation content.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

min.description: Generate a single fixed text value.

low.description: Generate a single arbitrary, non-recursive, inline node

high.description: Generate up to two arbitrary, non-recursive, inline nodes

max.description: Generate up to four arbitrary, non-recursive, inline nodes

annotation.$comment:
Most annotation will have a single paragraph but could have multiple
paragraphs, tables and even figures.

min.description: Generate a single paragraph (with no `Annotation` to avoid recursion).
